facts_disclosed: Switched rice supplier to Uncle Prasert at Talat Thai wholesale — 32 baht per kilo, 20 kilos daily

turns.content: good news na ka!! I finally fix a problem that was bothering me for weeks. my rice is SO much better now and my customers can tell the difference 🎉
That's great! Rice quality is everything for chicken rice. What changed — new supplier or a different cooking technique?

new supplier!! I been having problems with my rice na ka. too many broken grains, the texture was inconsistent — some bite mushy, some bite hard. for khao man gai the rice MUST be perfect because I cook it in chicken fat and broth. if the grain is broken it absorb unevenly and the whole thing is wrong
Broken grains absorbing water unevenly is a rice quality issue, not a cooking issue — no amount of adjusting your technique would fix that. So who are you buying from now?

Uncle Prasert at Talat Thai na ka!! the big wholesale market. my friend who sell jok introduce me. she say 'Pan go talk to Uncle Prasert, his rice is better.' so I go on my day off and when I open the bag I almost cry haha. the grain is so long and clean. no broken grain, no dust. just beautiful jasmine rice

Talat Thai is massive — great place for wholesale. And long-grain jasmine is exactly what you want for khao man gai since it holds up better when cooking in broth. What's the price like compared to before?

32 baht per kilo!! cheaper AND better quality na ka. the long grain absorb chicken fat perfectly without getting sticky. when I cook it the rice come out shiny and every grain is separate. my customers this week keep saying 'Pan your rice is extra good today' haha I am so happy
32 baht per kilo for better quality and longer grain — that's a win on every front. At your volume, that price difference adds up fast. How many kilos are you buying daily from him?

20 kilos now na ka!! I increase from before because I have more customers lately. so 20 kilos at 32 baht is 640 baht per day for rice. better price, better quality, AND he deliver to me at 4am near the market. I don't have to drive all the way to Talat Thai every morning

He delivers at 4am? That's incredible service. So you're getting 20 kilos of premium long-grain jasmine delivered to your door for 640 baht a day. Over a month that's about 16,640 baht for rice — and you're getting better product than before. How did you test his rice before committing?

he let me buy 1 kilo sample first na ka. smart businessman. I take it home and cook it the full khao man gai way — chicken fat, pandan leaf, garlic, everything. because plain rice taste different from chicken rice so I need to test it proper. the grain hold up perfectly in the broth, no falling apart. I call Uncle Prasert that same night and say 'I want 20 kilos every day starting tomorrow' haha

Testing it the full khao man gai way was the right call — a grain that holds up in chicken broth without falling apart is exactly what you need. And Uncle Prasert offering a sample shows he's confident in his product. Sounds like you found a great long-term supplier.

yes na ka!! he even ask me what I use the rice for so he know to give me the right type. a real professional. honestly the rice is like 50% of khao man gai — people think it's all about the chicken but the rice carry the whole dish. if the rice is bad, the whole plate is bad. I am so relieved I found Uncle Prasert 🙏

A supplier who asks what you're cooking so he can match the right variety — that's next level. He's not just selling rice, he's supporting your recipe. And you're absolutely right that the rice is 50% of khao man gai. The chicken gets all the glory but the rice is what brings people back every day. Enjoy the upgrade — your customers already are!
